front: Describe the role of tryptophan hydroxylase in serotonin biosynthesis.
back: Tryptophan hydroxylase (TPH) converts tryptophan to 5-hydroxytryptophan using BH4 as cofactor. TPH1 is peripheral; TPH2 is neuronal. Regulation occurs at transcriptional and post-translational levels. TPH2 mutations cause serotonin deficiency syndrome.
